name: Philippines (vice-presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the sun and corner stars of the coat of arms
the fringe and details of the coat of arms
details of the coat of arms

description: A white field defaced in the centre with the vice-presidential coat of arms — a design parallel to the presidential seal but without the ring of stars around the sun, the sun and corner stars rendered in blue rather than gold — framed with a knotted yellow-silk fringe.
history: First defined in 1947 under Executive Order No. 38 by President Manuel Roxas, which set the design as the presidential pattern with sun and corner stars rendered in blue on a white rectangular field. The current design was fixed by Executive Order No. 310, signed by President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo in 2004, which specified the rectangular white background, the omission of the ring of stars and the 1:2 proportion.

features.role: Coat of arms of the Vice President of the Philippines (sun and corner stars in blue, no ring of stars)
